briefdescription.para: User-controlled scope (not implemented at the moment)

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the PTI MetricsScope API that enables collecting GPU hardware metrics for individual GPU kernels submitted by the application. Such collection might be more practical for users than time-based collection.

MetricsScope operates in three phases:
Configuration
Collection
Metrics evaluation

Configuration flow:
Call ptiMetricsScopeEnable to create a scope collection handle
Call ptiMetricsGetDevices to get available devices (defined in pti_metrics.h)
Call ptiMetricsScopeConfigure to pass desired metrics for a specific device
Call ptiMetricsScopeQueryCollectionBufferSize to query the estimated buffer size
Call ptiMetricsScopeSetCollectionBufferSize to set the desired collection buffer size
Collection is initiated by calling ptiMetricsScopeStartCollection and ended by calling ptiMetricsScopeStopCollection.

For the Collection and Metrics evaluation phases, there are 2 types of buffers that MetricsScope deals with: Collection buffers - buffers that accumulate raw data during collection; mostly black boxes for users; one buffer contains data for one specific device; owned by PTI; buffer size (same for all) is setup by the user during the configuration phase; PTI allocates buffers on demand during collection

Metrics buffers - buffers where PTI populates requested metrics after collection has stopped, taking collection buffer(s) as input; owned by the user; contain a set of uniform records of pti_metrics_scope_record_t type

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize the scope collection handle Usage: Call this function first to create a scope collection handle before configuring metrics collection.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to store the scope collection handle

memberdef.argsstring: (pti_scope_collection_handle_t scope_collection_handle, pti_metrics_scope_mode_t collection_mode, pti_device_handle_t *devices_to_profile, uint32_t device_count, const char **metric_names, size_t metric_count)
memberdef.name: ptiMetricsScopeConfigure

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle obtained from ptiMetricsScopeEnable
Collection mode; currently only PTI_METRICS_SCOPE_AUTO_KERNEL is supported
Array of device handles for target devices; currently only one device per MetricsScope is supported
Number of devices in the devices_to_profile array
Array of metric names to collect
Number of metric names in the metric_names array

briefdescription.para: Query the estimated collection buffer size required for collecting metrics for the specified scope count.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Number of scopes to estimate collection buffer size
Pointer to store the estimated collection buffer size in bytes

briefdescription.para: Set the collection buffer size to be used during collection Note: As soon as the first such buffer is full, PTI will allocate a second one and so on.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Size of the collection buffer in bytes for later allocation
Scope collection handle
Scope collection handle

briefdescription.para: Disable MetricsScope and free all associated resources.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle to cleanup

briefdescription.para: Get the number of collection buffers available.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Pointer to store the number of available collection buffers

briefdescription.para: Get the collection buffer of the specified index and its size Note: The size might be handy for future usage when such buffers might be stored by the user for fully offline processing.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Index of the collection buffer to retrieve
Pointer to store the collection buffer address
Pointer to store the collection buffer size in bytes

briefdescription.para: Get information about the collection buffer.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Collection buffer to query properties for
props Pointer to store the collection buffer properties; user must not forget to initialize props->_struct_size prior to the call

briefdescription.para: Query for the required metrics buffer size for storing calculated metrics records.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the exact metrics buffer size needed to store all records from a collection buffer, including space for strings and metric values.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Collection buffer to query
Required metrics buffer size in bytes
Number of records that will be stored

memberdef.argsstring: (pti_scope_collection_handle_t scope_collection_handle, void *collection_buffer, void *metrics_buffer, size_t metrics_buffer_size, size_t *records_count)
memberdef.name: ptiMetricsScopeCalculateMetrics

briefdescription.para: Calculate metrics from collection data and populate into user-provided metrics buffer Usage: The user must first call ptiMetricsScopeQueryMetricsBufferSize to determine the required metrics buffer size, then allocate the metrics buffer and call this function.

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Collection buffer containing raw metrics data
User metrics buffer for storing records
Size of metrics_buffer in bytes
Number of records that will be/were written to the metrics buffer

briefdescription.para: Get metadata for user's metrics buffer in a scope collection This function provides type and unit information for all requested metrics. Call this function to get metadata per scope that applies to all records in all buffers.

detaileddescription.para: The metadata structure contains direct pointers to metric information stored within the scope collection handle. These pointers remain valid until ptiMetricsScopeDisable is called.

Usage:
User must set metadata->_struct_size = sizeof(pti_metrics_scope_record_metadata_t) before calling
Function populates metadata->_metrics_count and the three array pointers
No memory allocation is performed by this function
Returned pointers reference internal scope collection data (no copying)

parameterdescription.para: Scope collection handle
Metadata structure to populate
